retest_reason: Prompt optimization per soft-fail path: added Type Combinatorics + Security/Network Patterns guidance to SKILL.md Template 1 DIMENSION 1+2

exact_matches.description: HTTPDigest auto_error — detected as P0 by security subagent with precise evidence at http.py:L415-L419
truncation — detected at both routing.py:L534 and _compat.py:L392. Both truncation points identified.
convert_underscores=False propagation — detected as P1. Root cause: getattr default fallback at dependencies/utils.py:L766
Pydantic 2.11 internal API dependency — detected as P2 compatibility risk in _compat.py

close_matches.description: Union+Form type classification gap in analyze_param — detected as P1. Subagent correctly identified the union type handling concern in dependency analysis, though without pinpointing the exact Form+Union combinatorics code path.

false_negatives.why_missed: Subagent checked Annotated type propagation but did not identify the AfterValidator-specific pattern. AfterValidator is a Pydantic-specific validator lifecycle feature that requires domain-specific knowledge. This is the remaining gap even with optimized prompts — needs framework-specific rule files (v0.5 P3.1 guards.yml pattern).

analysis.optimization_impact: Type Combinatorics guidance (+8 lines in DIMENSION 1) directly enabled KB-3 and KB-4 detection. Security/Network Patterns guidance (+8 lines in DIMENSION 2) directly enabled KB-2 dual-point detection. Parameter flag propagation rule specifically caught the HTTPDigest/Bearer auto_error asymmetry.
analysis.remaining_gap: KB-5 (AfterValidator) requires Pydantic-specific domain knowledge. This is the same gap as security-best-practices skill's language-specific reference files. ADD would need a Python/Pydantic reference file (v0.5 scope).
analysis.scoped_audit_note: This is a scoped audit (3 modules). Full-repo audit with all modules would enable broader pattern detection. Results are directionally meaningful but scoped.
